Grinding dried lavender with a stone mortar and pestle, purple dust swirling into the air.
Stirring a bubbling cauldron with a wooden spoon, liquid shifting from green to gold.
Tying bundles of rosemary with twine, hanging them from a beam to dry.
Whispering to a seedling in a clay pot, watching it sprout upward at her words.
Pouring a glowing amber tincture through cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle.
Kneeling in the garden pulling weeds, dirt smudged on her hands and apron.
Reading a handwritten recipe book by candlelight, finger tracing faded instructions.
Tasting a brew from a wooden ladle, eyes closed, judging the herbal balance.
Gathering wild mushrooms in a wicker basket at the mossy forest edge.
Pressing flowers between the pages of a heavy leather-bound grimoire.
Feeding her familiar cat scraps while a potion simmers on the hearth.
Stringing dried chili peppers and garlic into a protective charm for the doorframe.
Crushing rose petals with honey in a marble bowl, releasing sweet fragrance.
Labeling small glass vials with handwritten tags, organizing them on wooden shelves.
Sprinkling salt in a circle around a bundle of burning sage and thyme.
Harvesting mint leaves by moonlight, placing them gently into a linen bag.
Dripping hot wax to seal a cork on a finished potion bottle.
Chopping fresh nettles with a curved knife on a scarred wooden cutting board.
Braiding dried wheat stalks and wildflowers into a harvest wreath for the wall.
Skimming foam from a simmering stock pot with a copper ladle, checking the color.
Planting fennel seeds in a terracotta pot, patting the soil down with muddy fingers.
Mashing elderberries in a wooden bowl, purple juice staining her fingertips.
Lighting bundled herbs on fire, letting smoke curl through the cottage rooms.
Measuring dried chamomile into a brass scale, adding pinches until it balances perfectly.
Stitching a sachet pouch closed with needle and thread, stuffed with protective herbs.
